---
title: "R neighbordt() bug with contained regions"
output: html_document
---

## The bug

`GenomicDistributions::calcNeighborDist()` uses an internal helper `neighbordt()`
that computes distances between consecutive sorted regions. Its formula reports
spurious positive distances when one region is fully contained within another.

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
library(GenomicDistributions)
library(gtars)
library(GenomicRanges)
```

## Demonstration with synthetic data

Region B is fully contained within Region A. The distance between them should be
zero or negative (they overlap), not positive.

```{r synthetic}
# A: chr1:100-500, B: chr1:200-350 (B fully inside A)
gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(100, 200), end = c(500, 350))
)
gr
```

```{r r_formula}
# R's neighbordt formula (1-based coords):
sorted_gr <- sort(gr)
ends <- end(sorted_gr)          # 500, 350
starts <- start(sorted_gr)      # 100, 200
widths_1b <- ends - starts + 1  # 401, 151

end_diff <- abs(diff(ends))     # abs(350 - 500) = 150
distance_r <- end_diff - widths_1b[-1]  # 150 - 151 = -1 -> clamped to 0

cat("R formula result:", end_diff, "-", widths_1b[2], "=", end_diff - widths_1b[2], "\n")
cat("After clamping negatives to 0:", max(0, end_diff - widths_1b[2]), "\n")
```

In this small example R clamps to 0 (then removes it). But when the contained region
is offset enough from A's end, R produces a positive value:

```{r contained_positive}
# A: chr1:100-1000, B: chr1:200-500 (B inside A, but B's end far from A's end)
gr2 <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(100, 200), end = c(1000, 500))
)

ends2 <- end(sort(gr2))          # 1000, 500
starts2 <- start(sort(gr2))      # 100, 200
widths2 <- ends2 - starts2 + 1   # 901, 301

end_diff2 <- abs(diff(ends2))    # abs(500 - 1000) = 500
dist2 <- end_diff2 - widths2[2]  # 500 - 301 = 199

cat("R reports distance:", dist2, "between two regions that COMPLETELY OVERLAP\n")
cat("Actual gap (start_B - end_A):", starts2[2] - ends2[1], "(negative = overlap)\n")
```

R says these regions are 199bp apart. They overlap by 800bp.

## Real-world occurrence in vistaEnhancers

```{r real_data}
data("vistaEnhancers", package = "episcope")

r_dists <- GenomicDistributions::calcNeighborDist(vistaEnhancers)
g_dists <- gtars::calcNeighborDist(vistaEnhancers)

cat("R count:    ", length(r_dists), "\n")
cat("gtars count:", length(g_dists), "\n")
```

```{r find_culprit}
# Find the extra value R produces
r_sorted <- sort(r_dists)
g_sorted <- sort(g_dists)

# R has exactly 1 value that gtars doesn't
r_tab <- table(r_dists)
g_tab <- table(g_dists)

extra_in_r <- setdiff(names(r_tab), names(g_tab))
cat("Values in R but not gtars:", extra_in_r, "\n")
```

```{r trace_bug}
# Trace which region pair produces the spurious distance
querydt <- GenomicDistributions:::grToDt(sort(vistaEnhancers))
querydts <- GenomicDistributions:::splitDataTable(querydt, "chr")

for (chrname in names(querydts)) {
  dt <- querydts[[chrname]]
  if (nrow(dt) <= 1) next
  endVect <- abs(dt[, diff(end)])
  regionWidth <- dt[, (end - start + 1)]
  dv <- endVect - regionWidth[-1]
  dv[dv < 0] <- 0
  idx <- which(dv == 230)
  if (length(idx) > 0) {
    for (i in idx) {
      cat("Found on", chrname, "at pair index", i, "\n")
      cat("  Region A:", dt$start[i], "-", dt$end[i], "\n")
      cat("  Region B:", dt$start[i+1], "-", dt$end[i+1], "\n")
      cat("  B starts INSIDE A and ends BEFORE A ends (fully contained)\n")
      cat("  R's abs(diff(end)):", endVect[i], "\n")
      cat("  R's width(B):", regionWidth[i+1], "\n")
      cat("  R's 'distance':", dv[i], "\n")
      cat("  Actual gap (start_B - end_A):", dt$start[i+1] - dt$end[i], "(overlap)\n")
    }
  }
}
```

## Why R's formula fails

`neighbordt()` computes:

```
endVect = abs(diff(end))
regionWidth = end - start + 1
distance = endVect - regionWidth[-1]
```

This is equivalent to `abs(end[i+1] - end[i]) - width(region[i+1])`. For
non-overlapping sorted regions, this simplifies to `start[i+1] - end[i] - 1`
(the gap in 1-based coords), which is correct.

But when region B is contained within A (sorted by start), `end[i+1] < end[i]`.
The `abs()` on `diff(end)` flips the sign, and the subtraction of B's width
can still yield a positive number. R then reports a "distance" that has no
biological meaning — it's computing the gap between the ends minus the width,
not the actual gap between the regions.

## How gtars fixes this

gtars uses `next.start - current.end` (in 0-based half-open coordinates). For
the contained-region case, this correctly produces a negative value (the regions
overlap), which is then filtered out.

```{r compare}
cat("All", length(g_dists), "gtars distances are real inter-region gaps\n")
cat("R reports", length(r_dists) - length(g_dists), "spurious distance(s)\n")
```

## calcNearestNeighbors: cross-chromosome contamination

R's `calcNearestNeighbors` has a second issue. It builds on the flattened output
of `calcNeighborDist`, treating the entire distance vector as a single sequence:

```r
# R's algorithm:
dist = calcNeighborDist(query)        # flat vector, zeros removed
upstream = dist[-length(dist)]
downstream = dist[-1]
nNeighbors = c(dist[1], pmin(upstream, downstream), dist[length(dist)])
```

This means the last distance on chromosome N and the first distance on
chromosome N+1 get paired as "neighbors." gtars computes nearest neighbors
per-region within each chromosome, which is biologically correct.

```{r nn_compare}
r_nn <- GenomicDistributions::calcNearestNeighbors(vistaEnhancers)
g_nn <- gtars::calcNearestNeighbors(vistaEnhancers)

cat("R nearest neighbors: ", length(r_nn), "values\n")
cat("gtars nearest neighbors:", length(g_nn), "values (one per region)\n")
cat("Total regions:", length(vistaEnhancers), "\n")
```
